mn29:0.2: The Longer Heartwood Simile Discourse Mahā Sāropama Sutta
mn29:1.2: that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Rājagaha on Vulture Peak mountain, not long after Devadatta had left.
mn29:1.3: Referring to Devadatta, the Blessed One addressed the monks:
mn29:2.1: “Monks, there is the case where a certain son of good family, out of conviction, goes forth from the home life into homelessness, (thinking,)
mn29:2.2: ‘I am beset by birth, by aging-&-death, by sorrows, lamentations, pains, distresses, & despairs, beset by stress, overcome with stress.
mn29:2.3: Perhaps the end of this entire mass of stress might be discerned!’
mn29:2.4: Having thus gone forth, he encounters gain, offerings, & fame.
mn29:2.5: He is gratified with that gain, offerings, & fame, his resolve fulfilled.
mn29:2.6: Because of that gain, offerings, & fame he exalts himself and disparages others:
mn29:2.7: ‘I am a person with gain, offerings, & fame, but these other monks are unknown & of little influence.’
mn29:2.8: He is intoxicated with that gain, offerings, & fame, heedless about it, and falls into heedlessness. Being heedless, he dwells in suffering & stress.
mn29:2.9: “Just as if a man in need of heartwood, seeking heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood—passing over the heartwood of a great standing tree possessed of heartwood, passing over the sapwood, passing over the inner bark, passing over the outer bark—cutting away the twigs & leaves, were to go off carrying them, thinking, ‘heartwood.’
mn29:2.10: A man with good eyesight, seeing him, would say,
mn29:2.11: ‘Ah, how this good man didn’t know heartwood, didn’t know sapwood, didn’t know inner bark, didn’t know outer bark, didn’t know twigs & leaves!
mn29:2.12: That’s why he, in need of heartwood, seeking heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood—passing over the heartwood of a great standing tree possessed of heartwood, passing over the sapwood, passing over the inner bark, passing over the outer bark—cutting away the twigs & leaves, went off carrying them, thinking, “heartwood.”
mn29:2.13: Whatever heartwood-business he had with heartwood, his purpose won’t be served.’
mn29:2.14: “In the same way, monks, there is the case where a certain son of good family, out of conviction, goes forth from the home life into homelessness, (thinking,)
mn29:2.15: ‘I am beset by birth, by aging-&-death, by sorrows, lamentations, pains, distresses, & despairs, beset by stress, overcome with stress.
mn29:2.16: Perhaps the end of this entire mass of stress might be discerned!’
mn29:2.17: Having thus gone forth, he encounters gain, offerings, & fame.
mn29:2.18: He is gratified with that gain, offerings, & fame, his resolve fulfilled.
mn29:2.19: Because of that gain, offerings, & fame he exalts himself and disparages others: ‘I am a person with gain, offerings, & fame, but these other monks are unknown & of little influence.’
mn29:2.20: He is intoxicated with that gain, offerings, & fame, heedless about it, and falls into heedlessness. Being heedless, he dwells in suffering & stress.
mn29:2.21: This, monks, is called a monk who grasps the twigs & leaves of the holy life,
mn29:2.22: and with that he falls short.
mn29:3.1: “And further, there is the case where a certain son of good family, out of conviction, goes forth from the home life into homelessness, (thinking,)
mn29:3.3: Perhaps the end of this entire mass of stress might be discerned!’
mn29:3.4: Having thus gone forth, he encounters gain, offerings, & fame.
mn29:3.5: He is not gratified with that gain, offerings, & fame, his resolve not fulfilled.
mn29:3.7: He is not intoxicated with that gain, offerings, & fame, not heedless about it, and does not fall into heedlessness.
mn29:3.8: Being heedful, he achieves consummation in virtue.
mn29:3.9: He is gratified with that consummation in virtue, his resolve fulfilled.
mn29:3.10: Because of that consummation in virtue he exalts himself and disparages others:
mn29:3.11: ‘I am a person of virtue, with fine qualities, but these other monks are unvirtuous, with evil qualities.’
mn29:3.12: He is intoxicated with that consummation in virtue, heedless about it, and falls into heedlessness. Being heedless, he dwells in suffering & stress.
mn29:3.13: “Just as if a man in need of heartwood, seeking heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood—passing over the heartwood of a great standing tree possessed of heartwood, passing over the sapwood, passing over the inner bark—cutting away the outer bark, were to go off carrying it, thinking, ‘heartwood.’
mn29:3.14: A man with good eyesight, seeing him, would say,
mn29:3.15: ‘Ah, how this good man didn’t know heartwood, didn’t know sapwood, didn’t know inner bark, didn’t know outer bark, didn’t know twigs & leaves!
mn29:3.16: That’s why he, in need of heartwood, seeking heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood—passing over the heartwood of a great standing tree possessed of heartwood, passing over the sapwood, passing over the inner bark—cutting away the outer bark, went off carrying it, thinking, “heartwood.”
mn29:3.17: Whatever heartwood-business he had with heartwood, his purpose won’t be served.’
mn29:4.1: “In the same way, monks, there is the case where a certain son of good family, out of conviction, goes forth from the home life into homelessness, (thinking,)
mn29:4.3: Perhaps the end of this entire mass of stress might be discerned!’
mn29:4.4: Having thus gone forth, he encounters gain, offerings, & fame.
mn29:4.5: He is not gratified with that gain, offerings, & fame, his resolve not fulfilled.
mn29:4.7: He is not intoxicated with that gain, offerings, & fame, not heedless about it, and does not fall into heedlessness.
mn29:4.8: Being heedful, he achieves consummation in virtue.
mn29:4.9: He is gratified with that consummation in virtue, his resolve fulfilled.
mn29:4.10: Because of that consummation in virtue he exalts himself and disparages others:
mn29:4.11: ‘I am a person of virtue, with fine qualities, but these other monks are unvirtuous, with evil qualities.’
mn29:4.12: He is intoxicated with that consummation in virtue, heedless about it, and falls into heedlessness. Being heedless, he dwells in suffering & stress.
mn29:4.13: This, monks, is called a monk who grasps the outer bark of the holy life,
mn29:4.14: and with that he falls short.
mn29:4.15: “And further, there is the case where a certain son of good family, out of conviction, goes forth from the home life into homelessness, (thinking,)
mn29:4.17: Perhaps the end of this entire mass of stress might be discerned!’
mn29:4.18: Having thus gone forth, he encounters gain, offerings, & fame.
mn29:4.19: He is not gratified with that gain, offerings, & fame, his resolve not fulfilled.
mn29:4.21: He is not intoxicated with that gain, offerings, & fame, not heedless about it, and does not fall into heedlessness. Being heedful, he achieves consummation in virtue.
mn29:4.22: He is gratified with that consummation in virtue, but his resolve is not fulfilled.
mn29:4.23: Because of that consummation in virtue he does not exalt himself or disparage others.
mn29:4.24: He is not intoxicated with that consummation in virtue, not heedless about it, and does not fall into heedlessness.
mn29:4.25: Being heedful, he achieves consummation in concentration.
mn29:4.26: He is gratified with that consummation in concentration, his resolve fulfilled.
mn29:4.27: Because of that consummation in concentration he exalts himself and disparages others:
mn29:4.28: ‘I am concentrated, my mind at singleness, but these other monks are unconcentrated, their minds scattered.’
mn29:4.29: He is intoxicated with that consummation in concentration, heedless about it, and falls into heedlessness. Being heedless, he dwells in suffering & stress.
mn29:4.30: “Just as if a man in need of heartwood, seeking heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood—passing over the heartwood of a great standing tree possessed of heartwood, passing over the sapwood—cutting away the inner bark, were to go off carrying it, thinking, ‘heartwood.’
mn29:4.31: A man with good eyesight, seeing him, would say, ‘Ah, how this good man didn’t know heartwood, didn’t know sapwood, didn’t know inner bark, didn’t know outer bark, didn’t know twigs & leaves!
mn29:4.32: That’s why he, in need of heartwood, seeking heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood—passing over the heartwood of a great standing tree possessed of heartwood, passing over the sapwood—cutting away the inner bark, went off carrying it, thinking, “heartwood.”
mn29:4.33: Whatever heartwood-business he had with heartwood, his purpose won’t be served.’
mn29:4.34: “In the same way, monks, there is the case where a certain son of good family, out of conviction, goes forth from the home life into homelessness, (thinking,)
mn29:4.36: Perhaps the end of this entire mass of stress might be discerned!’
mn29:4.37: Having thus gone forth, he encounters gain, offerings, & fame.
mn29:4.38: He is not gratified with that gain, offerings, & fame, his resolve not fulfilled.
mn29:4.40: He is not intoxicated with that gain, offerings, & fame, not heedless about it, and does not fall into heedlessness. Being heedful, he achieves consummation in virtue.
mn29:4.41: He is gratified with that consummation in virtue, but his resolve is not fulfilled.
mn29:4.42: Because of that consummation in virtue he does not exalt himself or disparage others.
mn29:4.43: He is not intoxicated with that consummation in virtue, not heedless about it, and does not fall into heedlessness. Being heedful, he achieves consummation in concentration.
mn29:4.44: He is gratified with that consummation in concentration, his resolve fulfilled.
mn29:4.45: Because of that consummation in concentration he exalts himself and disparages others:
mn29:4.46: ‘I am concentrated, my mind at singleness, but these other monks are unconcentrated, their minds scattered.’
mn29:4.47: He is intoxicated with that consummation in concentration, heedless about it, and falls into heedlessness. Being heedless, he dwells in suffering & stress.
mn29:4.48: This, monks, is called a monk who grasps the inner bark of the holy life,
mn29:4.49: and with that he falls short.
mn29:5.1: “And further, there is the case where a certain son of good family, out of conviction, goes forth from the home life into homelessness, (thinking,)
mn29:5.3: Perhaps the end of this entire mass of stress might be discerned!’
mn29:5.4: Having thus gone forth, he encounters gain, offerings, & fame.
mn29:5.5: He is not gratified with that gain, offerings, & fame, his resolve not fulfilled.
mn29:5.7: He is not intoxicated with that gain, offerings, & fame, not heedless about it, and does not fall into heedlessness.
mn29:5.8: Being heedful, he achieves consummation in virtue.
mn29:5.9: He is gratified with that consummation in virtue, but his resolve is not fulfilled.
mn29:5.10: Because of that consummation in virtue he does not exalt himself or disparage others.
mn29:5.11: He is not intoxicated with that consummation in virtue, not heedless about it, and does not fall into heedlessness. Being heedful, he achieves consummation in concentration.
mn29:5.12: He is gratified with that consummation in concentration, but his resolve is not fulfilled.
mn29:5.14: He is not intoxicated with that consummation in concentration, not heedless about it, and does not fall into heedlessness. Being heedful, he achieves knowledge & vision.
mn29:5.15: He is gratified with that knowledge & vision, his resolve fulfilled.
mn29:5.16: Because of that knowledge & vision he exalts himself and disparages others:
mn29:5.18: but these other monks dwell not knowing & not seeing.’
mn29:5.19: He is intoxicated with that knowledge & vision, heedless about it, and falls into heedlessness. Being heedless, he dwells in suffering & stress.
mn29:5.20: “Just as if a man in need of heartwood, seeking heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood—passing over the heartwood of a great standing tree possessed of heartwood—cutting away the sapwood, were to go off carrying it, thinking, ‘heartwood.’
mn29:5.21: A man with good eyesight, seeing him, would say,
mn29:5.22: ‘Ah, how this good man didn’t know heartwood, didn’t know sapwood, didn’t know inner bark, didn’t know outer bark, didn’t know twigs & leaves!
mn29:5.23: That’s why he, in need of heartwood, seeking heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood—passing over the heartwood of a great standing tree possessed of heartwood—cutting away the sapwood, went off carrying it, thinking, “heartwood.”
mn29:5.24: Whatever heartwood-business he had with heartwood, his purpose won’t be served.’
mn29:5.25: “In the same way, monks, there is the case where a certain son of good family, out of conviction, goes forth from the home life into homelessness, (thinking,)
mn29:5.27: Perhaps the end of this entire mass of stress might be discerned!’
mn29:5.28: Having thus gone forth, he encounters gain, offerings, & fame.
mn29:5.29: He is not gratified with that gain, offerings, & fame, his resolve not fulfilled.
mn29:5.31: He is not intoxicated with that gain, offerings, & fame, not heedless about it, and does not fall into heedlessness. Being heedful, he achieves consummation in virtue.
mn29:5.32: He is gratified with that consummation in virtue, but his resolve is not fulfilled.
mn29:5.33: Because of that consummation in virtue he does not exalt himself or disparage others.
mn29:5.34: He is not intoxicated with that consummation in virtue, not heedless about it, and does not fall into heedlessness. Being heedful, he achieves consummation in concentration.
mn29:5.35: He is gratified with that consummation in concentration, but his resolve is not fulfilled.
mn29:5.37: He is not intoxicated with that consummation in concentration, not heedless about it, and does not fall into heedlessness. Being heedful, he achieves knowledge & vision.
mn29:5.38: He is gratified with that knowledge & vision, his resolve fulfilled.
mn29:5.39: Because of that knowledge & vision he exalts himself and disparages others:
mn29:5.40: ‘I dwell knowing & seeing, but these other monks dwell not knowing & not seeing.’
mn29:5.41: He is intoxicated with that knowledge & vision, heedless about it, and falls into heedlessness. Being heedless, he dwells in suffering & stress.
mn29:5.42: This, monks, is called a monk who grasps the sapwood of the holy life,
mn29:5.43: and with that he falls short.
mn29:6.1: “And further, there is the case where a certain son of good family, out of conviction, goes forth from the home life into homelessness, (thinking,)
mn29:6.2: ‘I am beset by birth, by aging-&-death, by sorrows, lamentations, pains, distresses, & despairs, beset by stress, overcome with stress.
mn29:6.3: Perhaps the end of this entire mass of stress might be discerned!’
mn29:6.4: Having thus gone forth, he encounters gain, offerings, & fame.
mn29:6.5: He is not gratified with that gain, offerings, & fame, his resolve not fulfilled.
mn29:6.7: He is not intoxicated with that gain, offerings, & fame, not heedless about it, and does not fall into heedlessness. Being heedful, he achieves consummation in virtue.
mn29:6.8: He is gratified with that consummation in virtue, but his resolve is not fulfilled.
mn29:6.9: Because of that consummation in virtue he does not exalt himself or disparage others.
mn29:6.10: He is not intoxicated with that consummation in virtue, not heedless about it, and does not fall into heedlessness. Being heedful, he achieves consummation in concentration.
mn29:6.11: He is gratified with that consummation in concentration, but his resolve is not fulfilled.
mn29:6.13: He is not intoxicated with that consummation in concentration, not heedless about it, and does not fall into heedlessness. Being heedful, he achieves knowledge & vision.
mn29:6.14: He is gratified with that knowledge & vision, but his resolve is not fulfilled.
mn29:6.15: Because of that knowledge & vision he does not exalt himself or disparage others.
mn29:6.16: He is not intoxicated with that knowledge & vision, not heedless about it, and does not fall into heedlessness. Being heedful, he achieves a non-occasional liberation.
mn29:6.17: And it is impossible, monks, there is no opportunity, for that monk to fall from that non-occasional release.
mn29:6.18: “Just as if a man in need of heartwood, seeking heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood, cutting away just the heartwood of a great standing tree possessed of heartwood, were to go off carrying it, knowing, ‘heartwood.’
mn29:6.19: A man with good eyesight, seeing him, would say,
mn29:6.20: ‘Ah, how this good man did know heartwood, did know sapwood, did know inner bark, did know outer bark, did know twigs & leaves!
mn29:6.21: That’s why he, in need of heartwood, seeking heartwood, wandering in search of heartwood, cutting away just the heartwood of a great standing tree possessed of heartwood, were to go off carrying it, knowing, “heartwood.”
mn29:6.22: Whatever heartwood-business he had with heartwood, his purpose will be served.’
mn29:6.23: “In the same way, monks, there is the case where a certain son of good family, out of conviction, goes forth from the home life into homelessness, (thinking,)
mn29:6.24: ‘I am beset by birth, by aging-&-death, by sorrows, lamentations, pains, distresses, & despairs, beset by stress, overcome with stress.
mn29:6.25: Perhaps the end of this entire mass of stress might be discerned!’
mn29:6.26: Having thus gone forth, he encounters gain, offerings, & fame.
mn29:6.27: He is not gratified with that gain, offerings, & fame, his resolve not fulfilled.
mn29:6.29: He is not intoxicated with that gain, offerings, & fame, not heedless about it, and does not fall into heedlessness. Being heedful, he achieves consummation in virtue.
mn29:6.30: He is gratified with that consummation in virtue, but his resolve is not fulfilled.
mn29:6.31: Because of that consummation in virtue he does not exalt himself or disparage others.
mn29:6.32: He is not intoxicated with that consummation in virtue, not heedless about it, and does not fall into heedlessness. Being heedful, he achieves consummation in concentration.
mn29:6.33: He is gratified with that consummation in concentration, but his resolve is not fulfilled.
mn29:6.34: Because of that consummation in concentration he does not exalt himself or disparage others.
mn29:6.35: He is not intoxicated with that consummation in concentration, not heedless about it, and does not fall into heedlessness. Being heedful, he achieves knowledge & vision.
mn29:6.36: He is gratified with that knowledge & vision, but his resolve is not fulfilled.
mn29:6.37: Because of that knowledge & vision he does not exalt himself or disparage others.
mn29:6.38: He is not intoxicated with that knowledge & vision, not heedless about it, and does not fall into heedlessness. Being heedful, he achieves a non-occasional liberation.
mn29:6.39: And it is impossible, monks, there is no opportunity, for that monk to fall from that non-occasional release.
mn29:7.1: “Monks, this holy life doesn’t have as its reward gain, offerings, & fame, doesn’t have as its reward consummation of virtue, doesn’t have as its reward consummation of concentration, doesn’t have as its reward knowledge & vision,
mn29:7.3: That is the purpose of this holy life, that is its heartwood, that its final end.”
mn29:7.4: That is what the Blessed One said.
mn29:7.5: Gratified, the monks delighted in the Blessed One’s words.
